title: Corrective fix for B0050-review: Review Implement TUI Focus, Detail Scroll, And Mouse Behavior
description:
Blocked reason: Review found implementation work is still required: detail scroll state is cleared by no-op list navigation at selection boundaries, which violates the documented/spec'd reset-on-selection-change behavior.

Remaining work: Update list-navigation behavior so detail scroll only resets when the selected bead actually changes, then add regression tests for boundary no-op keyboard and/or mouse list navigation.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the minimum fix required to unblock B0050-review.
Update tests/docs as needed for the corrective change.
Leave a handoff summary that states how the parent bead can be retried.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated `TuiRuntimeState.move_selection()` to delegate through `select_index()` and preserve detail scroll on clamped boundary no-ops. Added runtime and app-level regression tests covering first-item keyboard and mouse list navigation that should not clear detail scroll.
handoff_summary.remaining: Parent review bead can be retried against this corrective patch. No additional implementation work is pending within this scope.
handoff_summary.risks: Targeted `unittest` coverage passed, but I did not run the full test suite. `python -m pytest ...` was not available in this environment because `pytest` is not installed.
handoff_summary.next_action: Retry B0050-review using this branch/worktree and verify the boundary no-op detail-scroll regression is closed.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: List keyboard and mouse navigation still share the same selection path, so downstream changes to selection/status messaging or panel routing should be validated against both input modes.

conflict_risks: List keyboard and mouse navigation still share the same selection path, so downstream changes to selection/status messaging or panel routing should be validated against both input modes.

execution_history.summary: Fixed TUI list navigation so detail scroll resets only when selection actually changes, and added regression tests for boundary no-op keyboard and mouse list navigation.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.

worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: The fix will likely touch shared list navigation paths used by keyboard and mouse handling. Future changes to panel headers, widget ids, or viewport sizing can still invalidate `_list_index_from_y`, `visible_*_height()`, and scroll routing unless they are updated together.
